---
title: "Where Quasi-Monte Carlo Theory and Practice Meet"
author: "Fred J. Hickernell"
subtitle: |
  Illinois Institute of Technology, hickernell@illinoistech.edu

  Joint work with several collaborators and the <span class="pkg">qmcpy</span> development team

  Supported in part by NSF-DMS-2316011 &nbsp; &nbsp;  ·  &nbsp; &nbsp; Thanks to the organizers

  Monte Carlo and Quasi-Monte Carlo Methods 2026 (MCQMC26), Edinburgh

  🔗 Slides: fjhickernell.github.io/MCQMC26/slides/MCQMC26.html

date: 2026-06-08
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"

metadata-files:
  - ../classlib/classlib/quarto/slides/research/research-slides.yml

execute:
  echo: false

format:
  revealjs:
    code-overflow: wrap
    footer: |
      Fred J. Hickernell · Illinois Institute of Technology · assisted by ChatGPT

---


# Quasi-Monte Carlo (QMC) 

\begin{equation*}
\overbrace{\Ex[f(\vX)] = \int_{[0,1]^d} f(\vx) \, \dif \vx}^{\alerttext{population mean}} 
\approx \underbrace{\frac 1n \sum_{i=0}^{n-1} f(\vx_i)}_{\alerttext{sample mean}}, \qquad \text{where }\vX \sim \Unif([0,1]^d)
\end{equation*}


Want 

- [Estimate/bound]{.alert} on error: $\displaystyle\biggl \lvert \int_{[0,1]^d} f(\vx) \, \dif \vx - \frac 1n \sum_{i=0}^{n-1} f(\vx_i) \biggr \rvert$

- [Reasonable cost]{.alert}: $\Order \bigl( n \, \$(f) + n^p \bigr)$ 
  - $\$(f)$ is cost of evaluating $f$ once
  - $\Order(n^p)$ is the cost of the error estimate/bound




## QMC Has Grown Over Several Decades

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}
\begin{multline*}
\overbrace{\Ex[f(\vX)] = \int_{[0,1]^d} f(\vx) \, \dif \vx}^{\alerttext{population mean}} \\
\approx \underbrace{\frac 1n \sum_{i=0}^{n-1} f(\vx_i)}_{\alerttext{sample mean}}
\end{multline*}

* [Practice]{.alert}
  - Motivates and illustrates theory 
  - Solves real problems

* [Theory]{.alert} justifies and explains practice


* [Foundation]{.alert} enables theory


:::

::: {.column .tree-col width="65%"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path(
    "../classlib/classlib/quarto/assets/images/qmc-tree/render_qmc_tree.py"
)

helper["render_qmc_tree"](
    show_group_labels=True,
)
```

:::

:::




## No QMC with roots only

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}

\begin{multline*}
\overbrace{\Ex[f(\vX)] = \int_{[0,1]^d} f(\vx) \, \dif \vx}^{\alerttext{population mean}} \\
\approx \underbrace{\frac 1n \sum_{i=0}^{n-1} f(\vx_i)}_{\alerttext{sample mean}}
\end{multline*}

&nbsp;

&nbsp;

&nbsp;

* [Foundation]{.alert} enables theory

  - Rooted in multiple disciplines 
  - Rare for a single person to have expertise in all of them

:::

::: {.column .tree-col width="65%"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path(
    "../classlib/classlib/quarto/assets/images/qmc-tree/render_qmc_tree.py"
)

helper["render_qmc_tree"](
    groups=["foundation"],
    group_labels=["foundation"],
    mask="root",
)
```

:::

:::

## A strong trunk supports the canopy

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}

\begin{multline*}
\overbrace{\Ex[f(\vX)] = \int_{[0,1]^d} f(\vx) \, \dif \vx}^{\alerttext{population mean}} \\
\approx \underbrace{\frac 1n \sum_{i=0}^{n-1} f(\vx_i)}_{\alerttext{sample mean}}
\end{multline*}


&nbsp;

* [Theory]{.alert} 

  - Draws on the foundation
  
  - Explains what may and may not work

  - Grows in response to suprises in practice

:::

::: {.column .tree-col width="65%"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path(
    "../classlib/classlib/quarto/assets/images/qmc-tree/render_qmc_tree.py"
)

helper["render_qmc_tree"](
    groups=["theory"],
    group_labels=["theory"],
    mask="canopy",
)
```

:::

:::


## Canopy is the most visible

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}

* [Practice]{.alert}
  - Solving problems with societal impact
  - Infrastructure for practitioners
  - Requires theory for justification
  - May not seem to align with theory at first

:::

::: {.column .tree-col width="65%"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path(
    "../classlib/classlib/quarto/assets/images/qmc-tree/render_qmc_tree.py"
)

helper["render_qmc_tree"](
    groups=["practice"],
    group_labels=["practice"],
)
```

:::

:::





# Low Discrepancy Sequence Constructions

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="65%"}

\begin{multline*}
\left\lvert \int_{[0,1]^d} f(\vx) \, \dif \vx - \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=0}^{n-1} f(\vx_i) \right\rvert
\\ \le \underbracket{\Dsc\bigl(\{\vx_i\}_{i=0}^{n-1}\bigr)}_{\Order(\alert{n^{-1+\delta}}) \text{ or \class{alert}{better}}} \, \lVert f \rVert_{\cf} \ \href{https://arxiv.org/abs/2502.03644}{\text{HKS26}}
\end{multline*}

Careful choices of $\vx_0, \vx_1, \ldots$ lead to low discrepancy, $\Dsc\bigl(\{\vx_i\}_{i=0}^{n-1}\bigr)$:

- [Digital nets and sequences]{.alert} [{{< meta papers.dicpil_2010_digital_nets_sequences.cite >}}]({{< meta papers.dicpil_2010_digital_nets_sequences.publisher_url >}})
- [Lattice rules]{.alert} [{{< meta papers.dickripil_2022_lattice_rules_book.cite >}}]({{< meta papers.dickripil_2022_lattice_rules_book.doi_url >}})
- [Halton]{.alert} [{{< meta papers.halton_1960_sequences.cite >}}]({{< meta papers.halton_1960_sequences.publisher_url >}})
- [Kronecker sequences]{.alert} [{{< meta papers.richtmyer_1951_sequences.cite >}}]({{< meta papers.richtmyer_1951_sequences.publisher_url >}})
- [Optimization and neural network based]{.alert} [{{< meta papers.fangmawin_2002_centered_l2.cite >}}]({{< meta papers.fangmawin_2002_centered_l2.publisher_url >}}), [{{< meta papers.ruskirbrolemrus_2024_mpmc.cite >}}]({{< meta papers.ruskirbrolemrus_2024_mpmc.doi_url >}}) 

:::


::: {.column .tree-col width="35%"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path(
    "../classlib/classlib/quarto/assets/images/qmc-tree/render_qmc_tree.py"
)

helper["render_qmc_tree"](
    labels=["sequence", "discrepancy", "missing"],
    tree_font_scale=1.5,
)
```


:::

:::

# Data-Driven Error Bounds

# Software Development and Dissemination

<!-- comment out for now, but will add back in later

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
#| eval: false

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path("../qmcsoftware/scripts/qmc_software_table.py")

helper["render_qmc_software_table"](
    "../qmcsoftware/data/qmc-software.yml",
    mode="slides",
    start=0,
    stop=10,
)
```

---

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
#| eval: false
import runpy

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path("../qmcsoftware/scripts/qmc_software_table.py")

helper["render_qmc_software_table"](
    "../qmcsoftware/data/qmc-software.yml",
    mode="slides",
    start=10,
    stop=20,
)

```
-->

# Collaboration and Community

## Thanks to many collaborators

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import yaml
from pathlib import Path
people = yaml.safe_load(Path("../data/collaborators.yml").read_text())
ncols = 6
img_width = "135px"
print(f'<div class="collab-grid" style="grid-template-columns: repeat({ncols}, 1fr);">')
for person in people:
    slug = person["slug"]
    caption = person["caption"]
    img = f"../assets/img/headshots/processed/{slug}.jpg"
    print(f'''
<div class="collab-card">
  <img src="{img}" alt="{caption}" style="width:{img_width};">
  <div class="collab-caption">{caption}</div>
</div>
''')

print("</div>")
```
---

# Future Directions

::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="35%"}

* [Practice]{.alert}
  - Solving problems with societal impact
  - Infrastructure for practitioners
  - Requires theory for justification
  - May not seem to align with theory at first

:::

::: {.column .tree-col width="65%"}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import runpy

helper = runpy.run_path(
    "../classlib/classlib/quarto/assets/images/qmc-tree/render_qmc_tree.py"
)

helper["render_qmc_tree"](
    groups=["foundation", "theory", "practice"],
    group_labels=["foundation", "theory", "practice"],
)
```

:::

:::


# References {#references}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis

import re
import yaml
from pathlib import Path

deck_path = Path("Plenary_MCQMC2026.qmd")
refs_path = Path("../classlib/classlib/quarto/metadata/hickernell-papers.yml")

deck_text = deck_path.read_text()
papers = yaml.safe_load(refs_path.read_text())["papers"]

used_keys = sorted(set(re.findall(r"papers\.([A-Za-z0-9_-]+)\.cite", deck_text)))

def sort_key(key):
    return papers[key].get("short", papers[key].get("full", "")).lower()

def best_links(p):
    links = []
    if p.get("doi_url"):
        links.append(f'[DOI]({p["doi_url"]})')
    if p.get("arxiv_url"):
        links.append(f'[arXiv]({p["arxiv_url"]})')
    if p.get("publisher_url"):
        links.append(f'[publisher]({p["publisher_url"]})')
    if p.get("project_url"):
        links.append(f'[link]({p["project_url"]})')
    return " · ".join(links)

print('::: {.refs}')

for key in sorted(used_keys, key=sort_key):
    p = papers[key]
    print()
    print(f'[{p["cite"]}]{{.ref-label}}')
    print(p["full"])
    links = best_links(p)
    if links:
        print(links)

print()
print(":::")
```
